title: hieracium maculatum subsp. leptodermum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. leptodermum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Hieracium maculatum subsp. leptodermum include its potential to reduce blood pressure, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by making a decoction with the roots and stems, to create teas, infusions, and tinctures for various health remedies.
